---
name: Add New Computing System
about: Propose a new physical computing system for the ontology
title: 'Add [System Name]'
labels: 'new-system'
assignees: ''
---

## System Information

**Name:**
*The name of the computing system*

**ID:**
*A unique identifier (lowercase, hyphenated)*

**Realizes:**
*What mathematical function or computation does this system realize? (e.g., "matrix multiplication", "shortest path")*

**Description:**
*Detailed description of how the system works, its physical principles, and computational approach*

## Classification

**Substrate:**
*Physical substrate(s) - select from: mechanical, electronic, optical, biological, chemical, quantum, thermodynamic, fluidics*

**Computation Model:**
*Computational paradigm(s) - select from: analog-continuous, register-machine, finite-automaton, stochastic-process, variational, reversible-computation, quantum-circuit, adiabatic-quantum, neural-network, molecular-self-assembly, dynamical-systems*

**Determinism:**
*Select one: deterministic, stochastic*

**Reversibility:**
*Select one: reversible, irreversible*

**Exactness:**
*Select one: exact, approximate, heuristic, exact-in-limit*

**Realization Type:**
*Select one: analogical, procedural, variational, statistical*

## Performance Characteristics

**Operations:**
*List supported operations (e.g., ADD, MULTIPLY, MINIMIZE, INTEGRATE)*

**Speed Category:**
*Select one: instantaneous, seconds, minutes, hours, days*

**Speed Notes:**
*Detailed description of timing characteristics*

**Scale Category:**
*Select one: single-op, small, medium, large, unbounded*

**Compute Capacity Notes:**
*Detailed description of computational capacity and scaling*

**Energy per Operation:**
*Energy consumption estimate with units (e.g., "~1 fJ (CMOS)", "~1 J (mechanical)")*

**Programming Model:**
*How is the system programmed? Select from: physical-setup, compile-to-physics, evolutionary, trained-readout, direct-program, analog-patch*

## Supporting Materials (Optional)

**Examples (URLs and descriptions):**
*Links to videos, demonstrations, papers showing the system in action*

**DIY Resources (URLs and descriptions):**
*Links to build-your-own guides, kits, or instructions*

## Additional Notes
*Any other relevant information, references, or context*
